children.heading: Beginning farmer or rancher
children.label: 1 Beginning farmer or rancher
children.text: The term “beginning farmer or rancher” has the meaning given the term in .section 1991(a) of this title

children.heading: Office
children.label: 2 Office
children.text: The term “Office” means the Office of Partnerships and Public Engagement established under this section.

children.heading: Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.label: 3 Socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher
children.text: The term “socially disadvantaged farmer or rancher” has the meaning given the term in section 2279(e) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: A
children.text: to improve access to programs of the Department;

children.label: i
children.text: small farms and ranches;

children.label: ii
children.text: beginning farmers or ranchers;

children.label: iii
children.text: socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers;

children.label: iv
children.text: limited resource producers; and

children.label: v
children.text: veteran farmers and ranchers; and

children.label: C
children.text: to promote youth outreach.

children.heading: Director
children.label: 2 Director
children.text: The Office shall be headed by a Director, to be appointed by the Secretary from among the competitive service.

establishing and monitoring the goals and objectives of the Department to increase participation in programs of the Department by small, beginning, socially disadvantaged, or veteran farmers or ranchers;
assessing the effectiveness of Department outreach programs;
developing and implementing a plan to coordinate outreach activities and services provided by the Department;
providing input to the agencies and offices on programmatic and policy decisions;

measuring outcomes of the programs and activities of the Department on small farms and ranches, beginning farmers or ranchers, veteran farmers or ranchers, and socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers programs;
recommending new initiatives and programs to the Secretary; and
carrying out any other related duties that the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish within the Office the Socially Disadvantaged Farmers Group.

children.label: A
children.text: shall carry out ; andsection 2279 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of activities described in section 2279(a) of this title, may conduct such activities through other agencies and offices of the Department.1

children.label: A
children.text: the Advisory Committee on Minority Farmers established under section 14009 of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008; and1

children.label: B
children.text: the position of Farmworker Coordinator established under subsection (f).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Socially Disadvantaged Farmers Group may carry out other duties to improve access to, and participation in, programs of the Department by socially disadvantaged farmers or ranchers, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Office of Outreach and Diversity
children.label: B Office of Outreach and Diversity
children.text: The Office of Partnerships and Public Engagement shall carry out the functions and duties of the Office of Outreach and Diversity carried out by the Assistant Secretary for Civil Rights as such functions and duties existed immediately before the date of the enactment of this section.

children.heading: Small Farms and Beginning Farmers and Ranchers Group
children.label: e Small Farms and Beginning Farmers and Ranchers Group

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish within the Office the Small Farms and Beginning Farmers and Ranchers Group.

children.heading: Oversee offices
children.label: A Oversee offices
children.text: The Small Farms and Beginning Farmers and Ranchers Group shall oversee the operations of the Office of Small Farms Coordination established by Departmental Regulation 9700–1 ().August 3, 2006

children.heading: Beginning farmer and rancher development program
children.label: B Beginning farmer and rancher development program
children.text: The Small Farms and Beginning Farmers and Ranchers Group shall consult with the National Institute for Food and Agriculture on the administration of the beginning farmer and rancher development grant program established under subsection (d) of .section 2279 of this title

children.heading: Advisory Committee for Beginning Farmers and Ranchers
children.label: C Advisory Committee for Beginning Farmers and Ranchers
children.text: The Small Farms and Beginning Farmers and Ranchers Group shall coordinate the activities of the Group with the Advisory Committee for Beginning Farmers and Ranchers established under section 5(b) of the Agricultural Credit Improvement Act of 1992 ( note; ).7 U.S.C. 1621Public Law 102–5541

children.heading: Other duties
children.label: D Other duties
children.text: The Small Farms and Beginning Farmers and Ranchers Group may carry out other duties to improve access to, and participation in, programs of the Department by small farms and ranches and beginning farmers or ranchers, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The Secretary shall establish within the Office the position of Farmworker Coordinator (referred to in this subsection as the “Coordinator”).

children.label: A
children.text: Assisting in administering the program established by .section 5177a of title 42

children.label: B
children.text: Serving as a liaison to community-based nonprofit organizations that represent and have demonstrated experience serving low-income migrant and seasonal farmworkers.

children.label: C
children.text: Coordinating with the Department, other Federal agencies, and State and local governments to ensure that farmworker needs are assessed and met during declared disasters and other emergencies.

children.label: D
children.text: Consulting within the Office and with other entities to better integrate farmworker perspectives, concerns, and interests into the ongoing programs of the Department.

children.label: E
children.text: Consulting with appropriate institutions on research, program improvements, or agricultural education opportunities that assist low-income and migrant seasonal farmworkers.

children.label: F
children.text: Assisting farmworkers in becoming agricultural producers or landowners.

children.label: A
children.text: such sums as are necessary for each of fiscal years 2009 through 2013; and

children.label: B
children.text: $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023.
